name: The Water Route (PR-A 218) - circular from Guillena
name_original: La Ruta del Agua (PR-A 218) – circular desde Guillena
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Guillena are set within a diverse landscape, nestled between the Huelva and Cala rivers at the foothills of the Sierra Morena mountains. This geographical position provides varied terrain, including riverine areas, forests, and mountainous inclines. The region features several natural attractions such as the Gergal and Cala reservoirs, and the Periurban El Gergal Park. Best touring cycling routes around Guillena The most popular touring cycling route is The Water Route (PR-A 218) - circular from Guillena, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through the Sierra Morena foothills, offering scenic views alongside the riverside and encompassing the Gergal Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Cantina – Water Trail loop from Guillena, a difficult 23.9 miles (38.5 km) path. This route provides a challenging experience through varied terrain, including forested sections and inclines. Local touring cyclists also love the Water Trail – La Cantina loop from Las Pajanosas, a 41.4 miles (66.6 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and the Sierra Morena foothills, often completed in about 5 hours 39 minutes. Touring cycling around Guillena is defined by riverine landscapes, forests, and mountainous inclines in the Sierra Morena foothills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Guillena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 650 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Guillena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Guillena?
A: Guillena offers a diverse selection of touring cycling routes, with over 25 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and challenging paths through the region's varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Guillena?
A: Touring cycling routes around Guillena feature a diverse landscape, nestled between the Huelva and Cala rivers at the foothills of the Sierra Morena mountains. You can expect varied terrain, including riverine areas, lush forests, and mountainous inclines, offering both scenic beauty and different levels of challenge.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Guillena?
A: Yes, Guillena offers routes suitable for beginners and families. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available, providing a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What is a popular moderate-difficulty touring cycling route in Guillena?
A: A popular moderate-difficulty route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987675582/the-water-route-pr-a-218-circular-from-guillena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Water Route (PR-A 218) - circular from Guillena&lt;/a&gt;. This 20.2-mile (32.5 km) trail winds through the Sierra Morena foothills, offering scenic views alongside the riverside and encompassing the Gergal Reservoir.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Trail – La Cantina loop from Las Pajanosas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 41.4-mile (66.6 km) trail. It leads through riverine landscapes and the Sierra Morena foothills, often taking around 5 hours 39 minutes to complete.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while cycling around Guillena?
A: The region boasts several natural attractions perfect for exploration. Cyclists can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gergal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2694489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Agua of the Gergal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. These areas offer picturesque backdrops and opportunities to enjoy the natural beauty of the Sierra Morena foothills.

Q: Are there historical or cultural landmarks to see on a touring cycling trip?
A: Yes, Guillena and its surroundings offer several historical and cultural points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/243856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alamillo Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4081520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amphitheatre of Italica&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3745522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of San Isidoro del Campo&lt;/a&gt;. Guillena itself has charming whitewashed houses and the remains of a castle integrated into its bullring.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about cycling in Guillena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Guillena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from riverine landscapes to mountainous inclines, and the scenic beauty of the Sierra Morena foothills.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes available?
A: For those seeking a longer journey, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guillena Town Hall – Castle of the Towers loop from Torre de la Reina&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial route. This difficult trail covers approximately 86.6 miles (139.4 km), offering an extensive touring experience through the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Guillena?
A: Guillena's climate makes it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable, while winters are mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Guillena?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Guillena are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20126376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cantina – Water Trail loop from Guillena&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a varied experience through forested sections and inclines.

Q: Are there routes that pass through urban parks or green spaces?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate green spaces. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40333065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alamillo Bridge – Alamillo Park loop from Torre de la Reina&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2616148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alamillo Park&lt;/a&gt;, offering a mix of natural and urban landscapes.
tips: The mythical bar where hikers eat after completing the Ruta del Agua, a beautiful path very close to Seville. At lunchtime, it is worth asking Paco - the owner of the place - for advice.
Views of the Gergal Reservoir and Riviera de Huelva. We can also observe some of the aquatic birds such as cormorants, grebes and others.
Casa de los Cazurros is one of the refuges during the Ruta del Agua - it has picnic areas.
Reservoir at the confluence of the Rivera de Huelva river with the Rivera de Cala river. Its exploitation began in 1979.

Guillena is a small town located in the province of Seville known for its rich history, diverse landscape and beautiful architecture.
The historic centre of the town retains much of its original charm and contains impressive buildings such as the church of Nuestra Señora de la Granada, the monastery of Santa Clara and the palace of the Counts of Torreblanca and the Sierra Norte de Sevilla, a large natural area that offers many possibilities.
Guillena is also an important centre for agriculture and livestock, with the production of olive oil, wine and citrus fruits, and a rich gastronomy with traditional dishes such as gazpacho and salmorejo.
